files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.AxiamAmqpConsumer
files.Summary:
AMQP event consumer: verify-before-handler HMAC gate plus the sdks/CONTRACT.md §8 ack/nack matrix, built on RabbitMQ.Client 7.x's async consumer API with automatic connection/topology recovery enabled (D-11).

NEVER hands an unverified delivery to the caller-supplied handler — runs first, and the handler call site is structurally unreachable until it returns true. The remaining outcomes follow the exact §8 decision matrix:

  • HMAC verification fails → BasicNackAsync(requeue: false) + a security-event log entry that never contains the received or expected HMAC value; the handler is never invoked.
  • HMAC verifies but the NEW-4 replay-protection gate () rejects the delivery — key_version below , a stale/future issued_at, or a replayed nonce — → BasicNackAsync(requeue: false) + a security-event log entry; the handler is never invoked.
  • Handler returns normally → BasicAckAsync.
  • Handler throws (poison message) → BasicNackAsync(requeue: false).
  • Handler throws any other exception (transient/retryable) → BasicNackAsync(requeue: true).

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.Hmac
files.Summary:
HMAC-SHA256 verify-before-handler primitive for inbound AMQP messages (sdks/CONTRACT.md §8, D-11).

Canonicalization is wire/insertion-order preserving, NEVER alphabetically sorted. The canonical Rust signer (crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs's sign_payload) signs the serde_json struct-declaration-order serialization of the message body (with hmac_signature absent). is backed by an ordered dictionary and preserves insertion order from parsing by default — mutates that same ordered map in place, preserving the relative order of all remaining keys. This is the single load-bearing property of this class: do NOT introduce an alphabetized copy, OrderBy/Sort, or a POCO deserialize-then-reserialize round-trip here — any of those would silently reorder fields and break every cross-language HMAC verification.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.PoisonMessageException
files.Summary: Poison-message sentinel a consumer-supplied handler throws to signal that the current delivery must be nacked without requeue, rather than the default nack-with-requeue applied to any other handler exception (D-11, sdks/CONTRACT.md §8). Mirrors the Go ErrDrop sentinel / Java ErrDrop exception used by the sibling SDKs.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.ReplayGuard
files.Summary: Enforces the NEW-4 (sdks/CONTRACT.md §8 "v2 — Replay Protection") consumer-side gates that run AFTER has already succeeded on a delivery:

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Amqp.ReplayMetadata
files.Summary: The key_version/nonce/issued_at fields extracted from an already HMAC-verified AMQP message body (sdks/CONTRACT.md §8 "v2 — Replay Protection", NEW-4). Extraction happens strictly AFTER has returned true — these values are meaningless (and MUST NOT be trusted) on an unverified body.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamAccessAttribute
files.Summary: Declarative, per-endpoint AXIAM authorization attribute (CONTRACT.md §11 require_access(action, resource[, scope]) — C# maps this to [AxiamAccess(...)]). Sugar over the existing "resource:action" policy string mechanism (/): this attribute serializes its // / into a structured policy name ("axiam::<action>::<resource>::<scope>::<param>") that recognizes and parses ALONGSIDE the legacy single-colon "resource:action" form (which remains fully supported — see its own remarks). Because this type derives from , it can be placed directly on a controller/action ([AxiamAccess("read", "documents")]) or passed to RequireAuthorization(IAuthorizeData) for minimal APIs — ASP.NET Core's own authorization pipeline combines it into a policy via Policy exactly like any other .

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamAuthMiddleware
files.Summary: ASP.NET Core authentication middleware (D-06, CONTRACT.md §10 "ASP.NET Core" row: app.UseMiddleware<AxiamAuthMiddleware>() in Program.cs). Mirrors sdks/java/.../spring/AxiamAuthenticationFilter.java's authoritative sequence (extract → verify → exp-check → cross-tenant-check → inject-identity → 401 JSON on failure), adapted to ASP.NET Core's / integration point — the direct analog of Java D-14's Spring SecurityContext integration.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamAuthorizationMiddlewareResultHandler
files.Summary: Custom that writes the standardized JSON error body (CONTRACT.md §10/§11, PATTERNS.md "Standardized JSON error body") for every authorization outcome: an unauthenticated result (401), a "lacks permission" result (403 — AxiamPolicyHandler left an unsatisfied, i.e. AuthzError), an unresolvable resource id (400 invalid_request, CONTRACT.md §11.2.3), and a transport failure while checking access (503 authz_unavailable, fail-closed per CONTRACT.md §11.2.5). On success, the pipeline continues exactly like the framework default.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamOptions
files.Summary: Typed Options-pattern configuration for / (D-07). Configured via services.AddAxiamAspNetCore(options => { ... }) — properties are mutable (set, not init) because the .NET Options pattern configures an already-constructed instance through an delegate.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamPolicyHandler
files.Summary: Routes [Authorize(Policy="resource:action")] (e.g. "documents:read") into AxiamClient.Authz.CheckAccessAsync (D-08). No direct Spring analog — Spring's sibling SDK uses method-security annotations rather than an /policy-provider pair, so this is the most novel file in the phase (built from RESEARCH.md Pattern 5, reusing the AuthzError→403 JSON-body convention from AxiamAuthenticationFilter.java lines 102-111).

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamPolicyProvider
files.Summary: Builds an containing a single for either the structured policy name produced by or the legacy policy name shaped "resource:action" (e.g. "documents:read") — the novel .NET-idiom file this phase introduces (D-08; no direct Spring/method-security equivalent, built from RESEARCH.md Pattern 5). Falls back to for every other policy name, so consumer-defined policies (registered via the normal AddAuthorization(options => options.AddPolicy(...)) path) keep working completely unmodified.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.AxiamRequirement
files.Summary: Authorization requirement carrying everything needs to run a single AXIAM access check (CONTRACT.md §11): the resource/action halves (legacy "resource:action" policy-string form, e.g. "documents:read", or the structured form — both funnel into this same type), plus the optional and the route value name used to resolve the target resource's UUID at request time. Built by .

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AspNetCore.ServiceCollectionExtensions
files.Summary: IServiceCollection DI extensions (D-07): registers the typed plus a single shared ; additionally wires the policy-based authorization surface (D-08). The raw app.UseMiddleware<AxiamAuthMiddleware>() form (CONTRACT.md §10) remains available and works with either registration path.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Auth.Jwk
files.Summary: A single JSON Web Key from the AXIAM organization-wide JWKS document (GET /oauth2/jwks). Shape confirmed against crates/axiam-oauth2/src/oidc.rs: kty=OKP, crv=Ed25519, x = base64url-encoded raw 32-byte Ed25519 public key, with a deterministic kid. Property names are explicitly pinned via to the exact lowercase wire field names — never rely on a naming-policy convention.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Auth.JwksDocument
files.Summary: The JWKS document envelope returned by GET /oauth2/jwks: { "keys": [...] }.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Auth.JwksVerifier
files.Summary: Local EdDSA (Ed25519) JWKS verification (D-02, CONTRACT.md §10 local-verification fast path). .NET has no native Ed25519/EdDSA support anywhere in System.Security.Cryptography (confirmed research finding — dotnet/runtime #14741/#63174 remain unimplemented through .NET 10 GA), so this class uses BouncyCastle.Cryptography's / directly — the single, well-vetted, verify-only crypto dependency D-02 permits.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Auth.LoginResult
files.Summary: Outcome of LoginAsync/VerifyMfaAsync (CONTRACT.md §1). MFA-required is an expected outcome represented as a flag — never thrown as an exception: callers MUST check before assuming a session was established. Mirrors the Java sibling's LoginResult (20-03) field shape.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Auth.RefreshGuard
files.Summary: Single-flight token-refresh guard (D-10, CONTRACT.md §9): guarantees exactly one in-flight refresh call at a time, regardless of how many concurrent callers observe an expired/near-expiry access token. One instance is constructed per AxiamClient and shared across the REST and gRPC transports (D-10's "one guard across REST + gRPC on one client" requirement) — never a second instance per transport.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Auth.TokenPair
files.Summary: An access/refresh token pair with an expiry timestamp. Both token values are wrapped in (CONTRACT.md §7, D-12) so neither can leak via , JSON serialization, or a log call.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.AxiamClient
files.Summary: The AXIAM C# SDK's public REST entry point (CONTRACT.md §1–§6, §9). The public constructor is the ONLY construction path — tenantId is a required, positional argument with no default and no overload that omits it (SC#1).

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.AuthError
files.Summary: Authentication failure: wrong credentials, expired session, MFA failure, or a 401 on refresh (CONTRACT.md §2, D-12). Always constructed via so REST and gRPC transports cannot drift on the error taxonomy.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.AuthzError
files.Summary: Authorization failure: the caller lacks permission for the requested operation (CONTRACT.md §2, D-12). Always constructed via so REST and gRPC transports cannot drift on the error taxonomy.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.ErrorMapper
files.Summary: Central status→error mapper (CONTRACT.md §2, D-12). The single source of truth for both the HTTP status table and the gRPC status-code table so the REST and gRPC transports cannot drift on the error taxonomy.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.NetworkError
files.Summary: Transport-level failure: connection refused, timeout, TLS error, DNS failure, or a server-side 5xx (CONTRACT.md §2).

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.Sensitive<T>
files.Summary: Wraps a token-carrying value so it can never be accidentally exposed via , System.Text.Json serialization, or a public getter (CONTRACT.md §7, D-12). Only SDK-internal code may construct an instance or read the wrapped value, via — there is intentionally no public accessor.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.SensitiveJsonConverter<T>
files.Summary: System.Text.Json converter for . Write always emits the redacted literal; Read is intentionally unsupported — a is a write-only-for-serialization type, so no wire format can ever deserialize a real value back into one.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.SensitiveJsonConverterFactory
files.Summary: Factory that produces the closed for a given . An open-generic converter referenced from a MUST be a : System.Text.Json cannot instantiate an open-generic JsonConverter<T> directly (it throws because the type still contains generic parameters).

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Core.TenantContext
files.Summary: Immutable tenant-identity value object required at AxiamClient construction time and propagated on every outgoing request (CONTRACT.md §5: X-Tenant-Id header on REST; x-tenant-id gRPC metadata in a later plan). There is no default-constructible/blank instance — the constructor throws a runtime guard backing the compile-time guarantee that AxiamClient's own tenant-required constructor provides (SC#1): AXIAM is multi-tenant and there is no default tenant.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Grpc.AuthInterceptor
files.Summary: Sync-safe authorization/x-tenant-id metadata injection on every outgoing gRPC call (CONTRACT.md §5), plus an UNAUTHENTICATED single-flight refresh-and-retry (§9, D-10) that drives the EXACT SAME shared the REST transport uses — never a second guard instance.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Grpc.AxiamGrpcAuthzClient.AccessCheck
files.Summary: A single authorization check request item for .

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Grpc.AxiamGrpcAuthzClient
files.Summary: gRPC authorization transport (CONTRACT.md §1/§2/§5/§9, D-10). Wraps ONE long-lived (via — strict TLS, §6) intercepted by an that shares the EXACT SAME /session 's REST transport uses — never a second guard instance.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Grpc.AxiamGrpcChannel
files.Summary: Constructs the SDK's ONE long-lived gRPC channel (D-10, CONTRACT.md §6) by reusing the EXACT SAME strict-TLS configuration the REST transport uses () — additive customCa chain-trust honored, never a TLS-certificate-validation bypass. The channel is disposed by whichever component constructs it (Grpc/AxiamGrpcAuthzClient.cs), mirroring the Java sibling's AutoCloseable GrpcAuthzClient and Go's ClientConn ownership model.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Grpc.UserInfo
files.Summary: The authenticated caller's OIDC-style identity claims, returned by (CONTRACT.md §1.1). The typed SDK-facing projection of the wire axiam.v1.GetUserInfoResponse — the low-latency gRPC counterpart of the server's REST GET /oauth2/userinfo endpoint.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Options.AxiamClientOptions
files.Summary: Typed, options-pattern configuration for AxiamClient (Claude's Discretion, 21-RESEARCH.md/21-CONTEXT.md D-07). and are required here for the future AddAxiam()/ IOptions<AxiamClientOptions> DI registration path (plan 21-06) — AxiamClient's own tenant-required constructor (this plan, SC#1) sources the tenant/base-URL from its own explicit positional parameters instead, so SC#1's compile-time guarantee never depends on whether an options object happens to be supplied at all.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Rest.AuthzRestClient.AccessCheck
files.Summary: A single authorization check request item for .

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Rest.AuthzRestClient
files.Summary: REST authorization client (CONTRACT.md §1, FND-04): CheckAccessAsync/ CanAsync/BatchCheckAsync over POST /api/v1/authz/check and POST /api/v1/authz/check/batch (mirrors the gRPC CheckAccess/BatchCheckAccess semantics a later plan wires up over the same shared session). Exposed as AxiamClient.Authz.

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Rest.AxiamHttpClientFactory
files.Summary: Builds the SDK-owned (D-09 default path) and configures the IHttpClientFactory-compatible alternative path (D-09 alt path, wired up by a later plan's AddAxiam()). Both paths guarantee the CONTRACT.md §4 persistent cookie jar and the §6 no-TLS-bypass policy — a caller-supplied handler can never silently drop either guarantee (client-override safety, Java D-27 analog).

files.Title: Axiam.Sdk.Rest.AxiamHttpMessageHandler
files.Summary: Cross-cutting REST transport concerns shared by every request an AxiamClient sends (CONTRACT.md §3/§5/§9): injects X-Tenant-Id (§5) and, when a session exists, Authorization: Bearer <access> read from the shared cookie jar; reads the axiam_csrf cookie the server sets and echoes it as the X-CSRF-Token request header on subsequent state-changing requests (§3 cookie double-submit — the real AXIAM server only ever delivers the CSRF value via a cookie, never a response header, so the cookie-jar read is the load-bearing path; the response-header capture is kept as defense-in-depth); and drives a single reactive 401→refresh→retry through the shared — never a second, independent guard, and never a retry loop (§9.3).

files.Title: Axiam.V1.AuthorizationReflection
files.Summary: Holder for reflection information generated from axiam/v1/authorization.proto

files.Title: Axiam.V1.AuthorizationService.AuthorizationServiceClient
files.Summary: Client for AuthorizationService

files.Title: Axiam.V1.AuthorizationService
files.Summary: Authorization service for low-latency access checks in service meshes.

files.Title: Axiam.V1.GetUserInfoRequest
files.Summary: Empty request — every parameter is read from the authorization bearer token, not from request fields.

files.Title: Axiam.V1.TokenReflection
files.Summary: Holder for reflection information generated from axiam/v1/token.proto

files.Title: Axiam.V1.TokenService.TokenServiceClient
files.Summary: Client for TokenService

files.Title: Axiam.V1.TokenService
files.Summary: Token service for JWT validation and introspection.

files.Title: Axiam.V1.UserInfoService.UserInfoServiceClient
files.Summary: Client for UserInfoService

files.Title: Axiam.V1.UserInfoService
files.Summary:
OIDC-style userinfo for the authenticated caller — the low-latency gRPC counterpart of the REST GET /oauth2/userinfo endpoint, analogous to Zitadel's zitadel.auth.v1.AuthService/GetMyUser.

Identity is derived entirely from the authorization: Bearer <access_token> metadata header (validated by the server's auth interceptor); the request body is empty. The returned claim set mirrors the REST endpoint exactly, including OIDC scope gating (see the field comments below).

files.Title: Axiam.V1.UserReflection
files.Summary: Holder for reflection information generated from axiam/v1/user.proto

files.Title: Axiam.V1.UserService.UserServiceClient
files.Summary: Client for UserService

files.Title: Axiam.V1.UserService
files.Summary: User service for lookups and credential validation.

files.Title: Axiam.V1.UserinfoReflection
files.Summary: Holder for reflection information generated from axiam/v1/userinfo.proto
